_comment: S3 DT Offset — Gage R&R for timing offset (DT) response (STUDY-SPEC §6). Redesigned 2026-06-06 per R&R-003 (GitHub #1): parts restricted to positive DT only; WSJT-X convention offset corrected.
name: DT offset
description: 10 parts × 2 appraisers × 3 trials. Parts restricted to DT ≥ 0.0 s (positive sweep) following R&R-003: OpenWSFZ cannot report negative DT offsets (decoder anchors at 0 for early-starting signals), so including negative parts inflated the App×Part interaction with a capability boundary rather than measurement noise. Both apps decode and accurately report DT in the positive range. Companion negative-DT decode-rate study: see s3b-dt-boundary.json. Drives: DT Gage R&R.
_wsjt_dt_correction_note: WSJT-X defines DT relative to the nominal FT8 TX start (~0.5–1.0 s into the 15 s slot) rather than the UTC slot boundary (harness convention, DT=0 ↔ signal starts at slot boundary). Run aa053a9 showed a systematic WSJT-X offset of ≈ −0.55 s across the full DT range. The analyser adds this value to the WSJT-X reported DT before computing bias/GR&R so the SS_appraiser term reflects genuine app-to-app measurement disagreement, not a calibration convention mismatch. See STUDY-SPEC §16 and R&R-003.
